Denied without prejudice to raising the issue with the trial judge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 16, 2014, Line 3,DEFENDANT G. MAZZERA COMPANY's Motion For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is denied. Failure to pay sanctions is not the type of court order violation for which a terminating sanction may be imposed. See Newland vs. Superior Court (1995) 40 CA 4th 608. That portion of the defendant's motion seeking a trial continuance is denied without prejudice. A motion for re-setting a trial date must be brought in the Department of the Presiding Judge. The Court notes plaintiff has made arrangements to complete her deposition on May 19. 2014 in San Francisco. Both parties' request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY D. MURPHY VS. MICHAEL YAKOVLEVICH KLESTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Timothy Murphy'S Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Defendant Daetwyler'S Memo Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 16, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY MURPHY's Motion To Strike And/Or Tax Defendant Daetwyler'S Memo Of Costs. The motion is continued to June 2, 2014. Plaintiff filed the motion to tax late. The court, however, will extend the filing period and entertain the motion to tax pursuant to CRC 3.1700(b)(3). CRC 8.25 is an appellate rule that is inapplicable to the instant proceeding. Defendant shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of the moving, opposing, and reply papers that formed the anti-SLAPP motion. The court needs to review those papers to determine what fees are reasonable. Defendant shall attach a cover letter to the anti-SLAPP motion that reflects the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ILONA BRUSIL VS. NIKOLAY BUSEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Further Respond To Requests To Produce Documents, Request For Special Interrogatories And Request For Admissions, And For An Order For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 16, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ILONA BRUSIL's Motion To Compel Defendants To Further Respond To Requests To Produce Documents, Request For Special Interrogatories And Request For Admissions, And For An Order For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories is partially granted and denied. The editing error in the discovery pleading definitions to whom the multiple discovery inquiries were directed was clarified in the "meet and confer" process. The responses should have been prepared and answered in that context. However, as to all forms of discovery categories directed to the defendants' use of the proceeds of the subject real property sale, the defendants' relevancy and privacy objections are sustained. When the "meet and confer" process was not successful in resolving the defendants' objection the total the number of interrogatories was burdensome, a motion for a protective order was required. Plaintiff's motion to compel response to the document request is granted to the extent defendants' shall serve an amended response to the document request without objection is granted. To the extent responsive documents have previously been produced, they need not be produced again. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted solely against defendant's counsel in the sum of $850.00 to be paid within 60 days of the date of this order. Any Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER RENNER et al VS. HOWE, ROBERT, TRUSTEE OF HOWE TRUST et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Discovery Resposnes By Pltfs Special Interrogatories [Set One]; Request For Production Of Documents And Things [Set One]; Special Interrogatories [Set One]; A Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance Pursuant To Ccp 2031.32; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 16, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTs STEPHANIE FAVRESSE, HOWE, ROBERT, TRUSTEE OF THE HOWE TRUST, HOWE, MARGERY H., TRUSTEE OF THE HOWE TRUST's Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories [Set One]; Request For Production Of Documents And Things [Set One]; Special Interrogatories [Set One]; A Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance Pursuant To Ccp 2031.32; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel is denied without prejudice to refiling to seek an adjudication on the merits of the disputed discovery issues after plaintiffs' late served supplemental responses have been reviewed and evaluated. In the discretion of the Court, based on review of the substantive and procedural "meet and confer" processes undertaken by the parties set forth in the declarations of counsel, defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of the filing fees for this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRISTINA MOE SALANOVA VS. VOXEO, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Protective Order, To Compel Production Of Dates For Depositions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, May 16, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF CRISTINA SALANOVA's Motion For Protective Order, To Compel Production Of Dates For Depositions And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties communicated to the Court subsequent to plaintiff's filing of the motion to compel that all of the substantive issues pertaining to the setting of multiple depositions have been resolved, and the motion for any substantive relief has been withdrawn. Remaining open is plaintiff's request for imposition of monetary sanctions as against defendant, Tropo, Inc., which is denied. The Court agrees with defendant Tropo's counsel the "meet and confer" process was not sufficiently exhausted as to that party so as to support a sanction award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
